Home How it works Why Velvoix Pilot Contact

Velvoix

From signal to clear care action

Velvoix helps healthcare organisations turn button calls, voice input and other care-related signals into explainable, prioritised and auditable care workflow.

Schedule a pilot conversation See how it works

Built for care environments where clarity, follow-up and reconstruction matter.

The challenge

When a signal is not yet a clear care action

Many systems can register that something happened, but they do not sufficiently support the next step: what is likely going on, how urgent is it, what follow-up fits the situation and how can the full sequence later be reconstructed properly?

Explainability

Why

Help button was triggered repeatedly within a short interval.

Based on triage rules and event context.

Primary signal

Repeated help request

Core observation

Follow-up is likely needed again.

Triage basis

Repeat trigger in a short interval and contextual signal data.

Operational follow-up

Recommended action

Go directly to the resident; check the situation.

Urgency

Direct

SLA status

SLA critical

Owner

Not yet assigned

Chain difference

Not from alert to interpretation, but from signal to controllable workflow

The value of Velvoix sits in the full chain: signals are not merely surfaced, but structured into an explainable, controllable and auditable operational outcome.

Comparison

Traditional chain

Signal
→
Alert
→
Manual interpretation

Velvoix chain

Each extra layer adds meaning and makes follow-up, escalation and audit controllable.

Signal

A button call, voice input or other care-related signal enters in a controlled way as the starting point for follow-up.

Less interpretation burden
Better prioritisation
Better reconstruction

How it works

From signal to workable event outcome

Velvoix does not just register signals, but enriches them with context, triage and follow-up oriented event behaviour, so care teams understand faster what needs attention and which follow-up makes sense.

Understand faster what needs attention
Recommended action becomes workable immediately
Audit and reconstruction remain intact

Event assembly

From raw input to a workable event with context, recommended action, status and audit.

Input received

Signal received

A button call, voice input or other care-related signal is received in a controlled way.

Context added

Context added

Repeat behaviour, history and situational context are added immediately.

Triage structured

Triage structured

Urgency and recommended action become visible in explainable care language.

Urgency Direct
Why Repeated within a short interval
Action Go directly to the resident; check the situation

Operational event created

Operational event created

Status, ownership and team follow-up become directly controllable.

Status Open
Ownership Not yet assigned
Follow-up Workable for team handling

Audit attached

Audit attached

Status flow and reconstruction remain traceable for evaluation and audit.

Why Velvoix is different

Why Velvoix works differently in operations

Velvoix is not built to simply pass signals through, but to structure care situations into explainable, controllable and auditable workflow.

01

Not a notification layer

Velvoix does not pass signals through as isolated alerts, but turns them into workable events with context, ownership and follow-up.

Less interpretation burden, tighter follow-up.

02

Explainable triage

Velvoix shows not only urgency, but also why something needs attention and which follow-up makes sense in care language.

Better prioritisation, more understandable action.

03

Event truth for workflow and audit

Status, ownership, escalation and reconstruction all build on one operational truth.

Consistent workflow, traceable reconstruction.

Technical core

Why Velvoix is technically hard to replace

The strength of Velvoix does not sit in one model, screen or signal source, but in a connected product core where triage, event formation, workflow, audit and governance reinforce each other in a controlled way.

01

Canonical event truth

Velvoix does not treat the alert or external system as the truth, but the event itself. That keeps status, escalation, follow-up and reconstruction consistent.

One operational truth instead of disconnected signals and derived UI logic.

02

Explainable triage chain

Triage produces not only urgency, but also context, recommended action and explainability in care language.

Understandable prioritisation instead of a black-box score.

03

Governance and control plane

Configuration, publication state and quality control are part of the product itself, not loose admin appendices.

Governable rollout without semantic drift.

04

Adapter architecture without vendor lock-in

Integrations provide input or receive output, but they do not define the product semantics of Velvoix.

Modular integration without polluting the core.

Together, these layers keep Velvoix from becoming a notification layer and turn it into an explainable, governable care platform.

Product showcase

A controlled view into the platform

Velvoix does not only surface signals, but brings triage, follow-up, management and reconstruction together in one care-oriented operational environment.

Not full screens, but carefully reconstructed desktop fragments that stay readable while showing more of the actual dashboard language.

Worklist

Desktop worklist with recommended action in view

Queue, urgency, resident context and follow-up stay readable in one operational dashboard view.

Worklist

Desktop worklist with recommended action in view

What this shows

A desktop worklist where queue, urgency, resident context and recommended action stay visible together.

Why this matters

This shows that Velvoix is not just a signal feed, but an operational workspace for teams.

Operational value

Teams can scan faster, understand faster and act with less interpretation burden.

Pilot and partnership

Explore with Velvoix what controllable care workflow delivers in practice

Velvoix is meant for healthcare organisations and partners that do not only want to receive signals, but primarily want to organise more clarity, better follow-up and better reconstruction. A pilot helps test how Velvoix fits existing care practice, team dynamics and operational processes.

Suitable for nursing homes, long-term care, operational care teams and strategic collaboration partners.

Schedule a pilot conversation Discuss collaboration
Pilot and partnership

In a pilot we explore

01

Operational clarity

See faster what needs attention, why that is the case and which follow-up makes sense.

02

Workable follow-up

Organise ownership, status and next action more clearly inside the daily care workflow.

03

Reconstruction and evaluation

Make status flow, escalation and retrospective visibility usable for quality improvement.

04

Fit and integration

Explore how Velvoix connects to teams, processes, devices and the existing environment.

For healthcare organisations
Focus on workload, follow-up, clarity and reconstruction.
For partners
Focus on fit, operational value and strengthening the care chain.
